etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 吉(吉) (jí) -> knot; tie
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread"), which symbolizes the physical threads being tied, with 吉(吉) (jí) providing the sound, leads to the concept of tying or forming knots, integral in ancient use of threads and cords.
etymology.simplification: 结 is the simplified form of 結, where 糸(糸) (mì, "silk; thread") is simplified to 纟, reducing the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 我们把绳子结在一起(我們把繩子結在一起)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bǎ shéngzi jié zài yīqǐ.

examples.chinese: 这条项链结得很紧(這條項鍊結得很緊)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo xiàngliàn jié de hěn jǐn.
examples.english: This necklace is tied very tightly.

examples.chinese: 他学会了如何把领带结成漂亮的结(他學會了如何把領帶結成漂亮的結)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xuéhuìle rúhé bǎ lǐngdài jié chéng piàoliang de jié.
examples.english: He learned how to tie a beautiful knot with his tie.
